content: One Health implementation requires: (1) Institutional mechanisms—joint committees, memoranda of understanding between ministries; (2) Integrated surveillance—shared data systems linking human, animal, and environmental monitoring; (3) Coordinated outbreak response—joint investigation teams, shared laboratory capacity; (4) Workforce development—cross-disciplinary training, joint exercises; (5) Research collaboration—prioritize transdisciplinary projects; (6) Policy alignment—harmonized regulations on antibiotic use, wildlife trade, food safety. Successful examples: rabies elimination through coordinated dog vaccination and human PEP, avian influenza control through poultry culling and farmer protection, AMR reduction through stewardship in human and veterinary sectors.
source: One Health Joint Plan of Action (WHO/FAO/OIE/WOAH), Lancet One Health Commission
